annotations.label: Asymptotic analysis

anchor.snippet: asymptotic analysis , also known as asymptotics , is the development and application of methods

annotations.note: Mathlib formalizes the central tools of asymptotic analysis (IsBigO/IsLittleO/IsTheta/IsEquivalent) but not the meta-concept of the discipline itself.

annotations.label: Function approximation example

anchor.snippet: An example of asymptotic analysis is function approximation.

annotations.note: Generic motivating example with no specific statement to formalize.

annotations.label: Prime number theorem example

anchor.snippet: The prime number theorem is an example of an important asymptotic result.

annotations.note: The PNT (π(x) ~ x/log x) is not present in Mathlib; only partial Chebyshev-type bounds like Chebyshev.eventually_primeCounting_le exist.

annotations.label: Asymptotic equivalence

anchor.snippet: Formally, given functions f ( x ) and g ( x ) , we define a binary relation

annotations.note: Asymptotics.IsEquivalent l u v is defined as (u - v) =o[l] v, generalizing the f/g→1 definition to arbitrary filters and normed groups.

annotations.label: Tilde notation and equivalence relation

anchor.snippet: The relation is an equivalence relation on the set of functions of x

annotations.note: The scoped notation `u ~[l] v` denotes IsEquivalent, with IsEquivalent.refl/symm/trans giving the equivalence-relation lemmas.

annotations.label: Alternative definition (little-o)

anchor.snippet: some authors use an alternative definition. The alternative definition, in little-o notation

annotations.note: Mathlib's IsEquivalent uses precisely the little-o definition: (u - v) =o[l] v.

annotations.label: Equivalence of definitions

anchor.snippet: This definition is equivalent to the prior definition if g ( x ) is not zero in some neighbourhood

annotations.note: isEquivalent_iff_tendsto_one gives IsEquivalent ↔ Tendsto (u/v) l (𝓝 1) under eventual nonvanishing of v.

annotations.label: Zero function never equivalent

anchor.snippet: The zero function,

annotations.note: Characterizes when a function is asymptotically equivalent to zero (precisely when it is eventually zero).

annotations.label: Partial ordering of little-o

anchor.snippet: The little-o relation has the partial ordering property defined as if

annotations.note: Transitivity of =o[l] is in Mathlib; the strict-partial-order Wikipedia property is captured by IsLittleO's irreflexive transitive behavior but not packaged as a Preorder/PartialOrder instance.

annotations.label: Reflexive, symmetric, transitive

anchor.snippet: Asymptotic equivalence has reflexive , symmetric and transitive properties.

annotations.note: IsEquivalent.refl, IsEquivalent.symm and IsEquivalent.trans collectively give the equivalence-relation properties.

annotations.label: Exponentiation preserves equivalence

anchor.snippet: If these functions are equivalent,

annotations.note: IsEquivalent.rpow shows u ~[l] v implies u^r ~[l] v^r for nonnegative v.

annotations.label: Quotients of equivalents

anchor.snippet: then the quotients are equivalent:

annotations.note: IsEquivalent.div proves t ~ u and v ~ w implies t/v ~ u/w in a normed field.

annotations.label: Integration and differentiation

anchor.snippet: Asymptotically equivalent functions remain asymptotically equivalent under integration if requirements related to convergence are met.

annotations.note: No Mathlib lemma stating asymptotic equivalence is preserved by integration/differentiation was located.

annotations.label: Stirling's approximation

anchor.snippet: Factorial

annotations.note: Mathlib's Stirling.factorial_isEquivalent_stirling expresses n! ~ √(2πn) (n/e)^n via Asymptotics.IsEquivalent.

annotations.label: Partition function asymptotic

anchor.snippet: Partition function For a positive integer n , the partition function

annotations.note: The Hardy–Ramanujan asymptotic for the integer partition function is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Airy function asymptotic

anchor.snippet: Airy function The Airy function, Ai( x ), is a solution of the differential equation

annotations.note: The Airy function is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hankel functions asymptotic

anchor.snippet: Hankel functions

annotations.note: Hankel functions are not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Asymptotic expansion

anchor.snippet: An asymptotic expansion of a function f ( x ) is in practice an expression of that function in terms of a series

annotations.note: The general notion of a Poincaré asymptotic expansion (with asymptotic scales) is not formalized as a definition in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Asymptotic scale

anchor.snippet: form an asymptotic scale

annotations.note: No definition of an asymptotic scale (sequence of gauge functions with φ_{n+1} = o(φ_n)) is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Gamma function expansion

anchor.snippet: Gamma function

annotations.note: The asymptotic series for Γ(z) (Stirling's series) is not in Mathlib, only the leading Stirling equivalent.

annotations.label: Exponential integral expansion

anchor.snippet: Exponential integral

annotations.note: The exponential integral Ei and its asymptotic expansion are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Error function expansion

anchor.snippet: Error function

annotations.note: The error function erf and its asymptotic expansion are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Worked example from formal power series

anchor.snippet: we might start with the formal power series

annotations.note: The illustrative worked example tying a formal power series to an asymptotic expansion is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Asymptotic distribution

anchor.snippet: an asymptotic distribution is a hypothetical distribution that is in a sense the "limiting" distribution

annotations.note: Mathlib has weak convergence of measures but no named definition labelled `asymptotic distribution`.

annotations.label: Asymptote

anchor.snippet: An asymptote is a straight line that a curve approaches but never meets or crosses.

annotations.note: No definition of a geometric asymptote of a curve appears in Mathlib.
